notes: The current official DC menu omits three frozen rotating specials and includes four current monthly specials missing from the baseline.
Official menu prose supports selected positive ingredient signals but is not a comprehensive allergen matrix, complete ingredient list, or cross-contact policy.
Several generated signals were derived from culinary product names rather than explicit allergen words in official menu text.
The generic website repair must reconcile rotating specials and restrict positive claims to explicit official menu wording.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Current restaurant-issued Washington, D.C. menu captured successfully.
Official ordering page confirmed the DC identity and restaurant-linked ordering surface.
Restaurant-linked Toast menu corroborated the current rotating-special lineup.
No comprehensive restaurant-issued allergen matrix, complete ingredients, legend, or cross-contact policy was located.
Older menu material did not provide a reliable current allergen authority.
Third-party menu was not used as allergen authority.

findings.summary: Chocolate S'mores, Peach Melba, and Strawberry Shortcake are stale; Peach Cobbler, Chocolate Birthday Cake, Cherry Pie, and Classic Cruller are current official specials omitted from the baseline.
Several baseline allergen claims rely on culinary product names such as brioche, quesadilla, cake, bun, mayo, cookie, or peanut butter rather than explicit restaurant-authored allergen wording.
The official menu contains selected positive ingredient prose but no comprehensive allergen matrix, complete ingredients, negative-claim coverage, or cross-contact policy.

repairs.summary: Reconcile current rotating specials and retain only positive allergen signals directly stated in official menu text.
